name: Twisted Fate
playstyleSummary: Twisted Fate is a card-throwing mage-marksman whose biggest strength is being able to teleport to almost anywhere on the map with his ultimate. Playing him feels like being a roaming threat: you poke with Wild Cards, line up a card ahead of time with Pick a Card, and once you hit level 6, you can appear in any lane to help secure a kill or defend an objective. He's less about dueling and more about map presence and picking off overextended enemies.
strengths: Destiny reveals the whole enemy team and lets him teleport almost anywhere on the map
Strong poke and wave clear with Wild Cards
Gold card from Pick a Card gives a reliable stun for picks
Flexible build that lets him adapt to different team compositions
weaknesses: Wild Cards is a skillshot that can miss if not lined up well
Needs to pre-select the right card before a fight for full value
Mediocre 1v1 potential without his cards ready
Squishy and reliant on picks rather than sustained damage

abilities.name: Loaded Dice
abilities.howToUseIt: Killing units gives you bonus gold at random. There's nothing to actively manage — it's simply extra gold from farming and takedowns.

abilities.name: Wild Cards
abilities.howToUseIt: Throws three cards in a line for poke and wave clear. Line it up to catch multiple enemies or minions at once for extra value.

abilities.name: Pick a Card
abilities.howToUseIt: Choose one of three cards ahead of time to empower your next attack: the gold card stuns, the red card deals bonus damage and reveals, and the blue card restores mana. Pick the gold card before engaging to set up a stun.

abilities.name: Stacked Deck
abilities.howToUseIt: Every 4th attack deals bonus damage and speeds up your attacks. Weave in autos whenever it's safe to keep this rolling and maximize your damage.

abilities.name: Destiny
abilities.howToUseIt: Reveals every enemy champion on the map and lets you teleport to any location for a few seconds. Use it to pick off a low-health enemy across the map, set up a gank, or teleport in for a teamfight.

earlyGamePlan: Poke the enemy laner with Wild Cards while farming with autos to build up Stacked Deck. Keep a card pre-selected with Pick a Card in case a fight breaks out, and once you hit level 6, look for chances to use Destiny to help another lane or punish an overextended enemy.
quickTips: Pre-select the gold card with Pick a Card before you plan to engage, so the stun is ready
Use Wild Cards to poke and clear the wave at the same time when you can line it up
Save Destiny to snipe kills across the map or to teleport into a fight your team is starting
Weave in autos to keep Stacked Deck's bonus damage active
Roam with Destiny once you have priority in your own lane
Watch the enemy's health bars across the map since Destiny reveals everyone briefly
whatToExpect: Enemy Twisted Fate can teleport to any lane in seconds with Destiny, which also reveals your whole team, so play a little safer and watch the minimap once he hits level 6. His gold card stun is dangerous when he engages, so back off if you see him line up Pick a Card before approaching.

glossary.definition: Chip damage dealt from a safe range to whittle enemies down before a bigger fight.
Leaving your lane temporarily to help fight or gank in another part of the map.
The ability to quickly kill a wave of minions.
Isolating and killing a single enemy champion, usually away from their team.
An ability that must be aimed and can miss if the target moves out of its path.
Initiating a fight by committing an ability or your positioning first.
Ambushing an enemy in a lane with extra teammates, usually from a jungle or side path.
A fight involving most or all champions from both teams at once.
Pushed or positioned too far from safety, such as too far from teammates or turret protection.
